creator: chess@chessgriffin.com
text:
Yes, I saw that in the commit history for this port.  The patch worked
when I submitted it, but I see that the author did another update on
November 11 to some files in the tarball itself, so once again the
checksums don't match.  :-/

I would be happy to submit another patch, but I guess that is just
postponing the inevitable.  I see on the ipbt website that there is a
subversion repository; would it make sense to grab a snapshot from
subversion on a set periodic basis and host a svn-based distfile on
freebsd.org instead?   Sort of like what Marcus does with sysutils/hal? 
Just an idea -- not sure if that would be better or worse than the current
situation.

Anyway, if there is anything I can to do help, please let me know.

Thanks-
Chess

-- 
Chess Griffin
GPG Public Key:  0x0C7558C3
http://www.chessgriffin.com
